classes.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.__init__
documentation: Sets default variables for the library

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.click_element
documentation:
Performs a single click on a given element. Used only for buttons, tabs and menu items.

In case you want to change a value of an element like checkboxes of selecting an option in dropdown lists,
use `select checkbox` or `select from list by label` instead.

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.click_toolbar_button
documentation:
Clicks a button of a toolbar within a GridView 'table_id' which is contained within a shell object.
Use the Scripting tracker recorder to find the 'button_id' of the button to click

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.connect_to_existing_connection
documentation:
Connects to an open connection. If the connection matches the given connection_name, the session is connected
to this connection.

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.change_session
documentation: Function to change session

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.check_busy_other_session
documentation: Function to check if session is busy or not

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.select_checkbox_other_session
documentation:
Selects checkbox identified by locator.
Does nothing if the checkbox is already selected  in other session

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.unselect_checkbox_other_session
documentation:
Removes selection of checkbox identified by locator.
Does nothing if the checkbox is not selected in other session

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.set_focus_other_session
documentation: Sets the focus to the given element.

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.doubleclick_element_other_session
documentation: Performs a double-click on a given element. Used only for shell objects.

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.click_element_other_session
documentation:
Performs a single click on a given element. Used only for buttons, tabs and menu items.

In case you want to change a value of an element like checkboxes of selecting an option in dropdown lists,
use `select checkbox` or `select from list by label` instead.

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.get_element_type_other_session
documentation: Returns the Sap element type for the given element.

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.input_text_other_session
documentation:
Inserts the given text into the text field identified by locator.
Use keyword `input password` to insert a password in a text field.

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.select_radio_button_other_session
documentation: Sets radio button to the specified value.

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.click_element_other_session_process
documentation: click element to other session in multithread or different process

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.send_vkey_other_session_process
documentation: send vkey to other session in multithread or different process

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.connect_to_session
documentation:
Connects to an open session SAP.

See `Opening a connection / Before running tests` for details about requirements before connecting to a session.

Optionally `set explicit wait` can be used to set the explicit wait time.

*Examples*:
| *Keyword*             | *Attributes*          |
| connect to session    |                       |
| connect to session    | 3                     |
| connect to session    | explicit_wait=500ms   |

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.disable_screenshots_on_error
documentation: Disables automatic screenshots on error.

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.doubleclick_element
documentation: Performs a double-click on a given element. Used only for shell objects.

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.element_should_be_present
documentation: Checks whether an element is present on the screen.

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.element_value_should_be
documentation:
Checks whether the element value is the same as the expected value.
The possible expected values depend on the type of element (see usage).

 Usage:
 | *Element type*   | *possible values*                 |
 | textfield        | text                              |
 | label            | text                              |
 | checkbox         | checked / unchecked               |
 | radiobutton      | checked / unchecked               |
 | combobox         | text of the option to be expected |

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.element_value_should_contain
documentation:
Checks whether the element value contains the expected value.
The possible expected values depend on the type of element (see usage).

 Usage:
 | *Element type*   | *possible values*                 |
 | textfield        | text                              |
 | label            | text                              |
 | combobox         | text of the option to be expected |

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.enable_screenshots_on_error
documentation: Enables automatic screenshots on error.

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.get_cell_value
documentation: Returns the cell value for the specified cell.

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.get_element_location
documentation: Returns the Sap element location for the given element.

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.get_element_type
documentation: Returns the Sap element type for the given element.

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.get_row_count
documentation: Returns the number of rows found in the specified table.

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.get_scroll_position
documentation: Returns the scroll position of the scrollbar of an element 'element_id' that is contained within a shell object.

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.get_value
documentation:
Gets the value of the given element. The possible return values depend on the type of element (see Return values).

Return values:
| *Element type*   | *Return values*                   |
| textfield        | text                              |
| label            | text                              |
| checkbox         | checked / unchecked               |
| radiobutton      | checked / unchecked               |
| combobox         | text of the selected option       |
| guibutton        | text                              |
| guititlebar      | text                              |
| guistatusbar     | text                              |
| guitab           | text                              |

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.get_window_title
documentation: Retrieves the window title of the given window.

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.input_password
documentation:
Inserts the given password into the text field identified by locator.
The password is not recorded in the log.

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.input_text
documentation:
Inserts the given text into the text field identified by locator.
Use keyword `input password` to insert a password in a text field.

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.maximize_window
documentation: Maximizes the SapGui window.

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.open_connection
documentation: Opens a connection to the given connection name. Be sure to provide the full connection name, including the bracket part.

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.run_transaction
documentation: Runs a Sap transaction. An error is given when an unknown transaction is specified.

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.scroll
documentation:
Scrolls the scrollbar of an element 'element_id' that is contained within a shell object.
'Position' is the number of rows to scroll.

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.select_checkbox
documentation:
Selects checkbox identified by locator.
Does nothing if the checkbox is already selected.

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.select_context_menu_item
documentation: Selects an item from the context menu by clicking a button or right-clicking in the node context menu.

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.select_from_list_by_label
documentation: Selects the specified option from the selection list.

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.select_node
documentation:
Selects a node of a TableTreeControl 'tree_id' which is contained within a shell object.

Use the Scripting tracker recorder to find the 'node_id' of the node.
Expand can be set to True to expand the node. If the node cannot be expanded, no error is given.

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.select_node_link
documentation:
Selects a link of a TableTreeControl 'tree_id' which is contained within a shell object.

Use the Scripting tracker recorder to find the 'link_id1' and 'link_id2' of the link to select.

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.select_radio_button
documentation: Sets radio button to the specified value.

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.select_table_column
documentation:
Selects an entire column of a GridView 'table_id' which is contained within a shell object.

Use the Scripting tracker recorder to find the 'column_id' of the column to select.

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.select_table_row
documentation:
Selects an entire row of a table. This can either be a TableControl or a GridView 'table_id'
which is contained within a shell object. The row is an index to select the row, starting from 0.

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.send_vkey
documentation:
Sends a SAP virtual key combination to the window, not into an element.
If you want to send a value to a text field, use `input text` instead.

To send a vkey, you can either use te *VKey ID* or the *Key combination*.

Sap Virtual Keys (on Windows)
| *VKey ID* | *Key combination*     | *VKey ID* | *Key combination*     | *VKey ID* | *Key combination*     |
| *0*       | Enter                 | *26*      | Ctrl + F2             | *72*      | Ctrl + A              |
| *1*       | F1                    | *27*      | Ctrl + F3             | *73*      | Ctrl + D              |
| *2*       | F2                    | *28*      | Ctrl + F4             | *74*      | Ctrl + N              |
| *3*       | F3                    | *29*      | Ctrl + F5             | *75*      | Ctrl + O              |
| *4*       | F4                    | *30*      | Ctrl + F6             | *76*      | Shift + Del           |
| *5*       | F5                    | *31*      | Ctrl + F7             | *77*      | Ctrl + Ins            |
| *6*       | F6                    | *32*      | Ctrl + F8             | *78*      | Shift + Ins           |
| *7*       | F7                    | *33*      | Ctrl + F9             | *79*      | Alt + Backspace       |
| *8*       | F8                    | *34*      | Ctrl + F10            | *80*      | Ctrl + Page Up        |
| *9*       | F9                    | *35*      | Ctrl + F11            | *81*      | Page Up               |
| *10*      | F10                   | *36*      | Ctrl + F12            | *82*      | Page Down             |
| *11*      | F11 or Ctrl + S       | *37*      | Ctrl + Shift + F1     | *83*      | Ctrl + Page Down      |
| *12*      | F12 or ESC            | *38*      | Ctrl + Shift + F2     | *84*      | Ctrl + G              |
| *14*      | Shift + F2            | *39*      | Ctrl + Shift + F3     | *85*      | Ctrl + R              |
| *15*      | Shift + F3            | *40*      | Ctrl + Shift + F4     | *86*      | Ctrl + P              |
| *16*      | Shift + F4            | *41*      | Ctrl + Shift + F5     | *87*      | Ctrl + B              |
| *17*      | Shift + F5            | *42*      | Ctrl + Shift + F6     | *88*      | Ctrl + K              |
| *18*      | Shift + F6            | *43*      | Ctrl + Shift + F7     | *89*      | Ctrl + T              |
| *19*      | Shift + F7            | *44*      | Ctrl + Shift + F8     | *90*      | Ctrl + Y              |
| *20*      | Shift + F8            | *45*      | Ctrl + Shift + F9     | *91*      | Ctrl + X              |
| *21*      | Shift + F9            | *46*      | Ctrl + Shift + F10    | *92*      | Ctrl + C              |
| *22*      | Ctrl + Shift + 0      | *47*      | Ctrl + Shift + F11    | *93*      | Ctrl + V              |
| *23*      | Shift + F11           | *48*      | Ctrl + Shift + F12    | *94*      | Shift + F10           |
| *24*      | Shift + F12           | *70*      | Ctrl + E              | *97*      | Ctrl + #              |
| *25*      | Ctrl + F1             | *71*      | Ctrl + F              |           |                       |

Examples:
| *Keyword*     | *Attributes*      |           |
| send_vkey     | 8                 |           |
| send_vkey     | Ctrl + Shift + F1 |           |
| send_vkey     | Ctrl + F7         | window=1  |

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.set_cell_value
documentation:
Sets the cell value for the specified cell of a GridView 'table_id' which is contained within a shell object.

Use the Scripting tracker recorder to find the 'col_id' of the cell to set.

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.set_explicit_wait
documentation:
Sets the delay time that is waited after each SapGui keyword.

The value can be given as a number that is considered to be seconds or as a human-readable string like 1 second
or 700 ms.

This functionality is designed to be used for demonstration and debugging purposes. It is not advised to use
this keyword to wait for an element to appear or function to finish.

 *Possible time formats:*
| miliseconds       | milliseconds, millisecond, millis, ms |
| seconds           | seconds, second, secs, sec, s         |
| minutes           | minutes, minute, mins, min, m         |

 *Example:*
| *Keyword*         | *Attributes*      |
| Set explicit wait | 1                 |
| Set explicit wait | 3 seconds         |
| Set explicit wait | 500 ms            |

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.set_focus
documentation: Sets the focus to the given element.

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.take_screenshot
documentation:
Takes a screenshot, only if 'screenshots on error' has been enabled,
either at import of with keyword `enable screenshots on error`.

This keyword uses Robots' internal `Screenshot` library.

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.unselect_checkbox
documentation:
Removes selection of checkbox identified by locator.
Does nothing if the checkbox is not selected.

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.get_all_window_title
documentation: Fungsi untuk mendapatkan title dari window semua session

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.close_sap
documentation:
Fungsi untuk melakukan close SAP

contoh penggunaan :
    Close Sap

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.shell_content_dynamic_selection
documentation:
Script untuk interaksi dengan sheell element pada SAP GUI pada dynamic selection
ada beberapa interaksi yang dapat dilakukan yaitu select, top, expand, double klik, presstoolbar dan list_tree

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.shell_content_element
documentation:
Script untuk interaksi dengan sheell element pada SAP GUI, terutama untuk pada tabel data,
ada dua interaksi yang bisa dilakukan yaitu get (mengambil data, text value) dan click (melakukan double klik pada sel terpilih)

methods.name: AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.AISAPLibrary.login_sap

documentation:
The SapGuiLibrary is a library that enables users to create tests for the Sap Gui application

The library uses the Sap Scripting Engine, therefore Scripting must be enabled in Sap in order for this library to work.

= Opening a connection / Before running tests =

First of all, you have to *make sure the Sap Logon Pad is started*. You can automate this process by using the
AutoIT library or the Process Library.

After the Sap Login Pad is started, you can connect to the Sap Session using the keyword `connect to session`.

If you have a successful connection you can use `Open Connection` to open a new connection from the Sap Logon Pad
or `Connect To Existing Connection` to connect to a connection that is already open.

= Locating or specifying elements =

You need to specify elements starting from the window ID, for example, wnd[0]/tbar[1]/btn[8]. In some cases the SAP
ID contains backslashes. Make sure you escape these backslashes by adding another backslash in front of it.

= Screenshots (on error) =

The SapGUILibrary offers an option for automatic screenshots on error.
Default this option is enabled, use keyword `disable screenshots on error` to skip the screenshot functionality.
Alternatively, this option can be set at import.
